headline: Chernobyl cloud stopped at the border: The making of a uniquely French myth
description: The legend of the radioactive cloud that supposedly never crossed into France has left a lasting mark on the national imagination. Yet that narrative has been rewritten many times over the decades.

headline: King Charles III meets Trump in bid to salvage UK-US ties
Senegal's anti-gay law is a smokescreen for the country's powerless government
A new ultrasound technique promises to spare patients from surgery for digestive endometriosis
description: After several studies, a clinical trial is now underway to test the use of ultrasound to treat rectal endometriosis nodules, a development that is bringing hope to patients.

headline: France's National Center of Stage Costume showcases exceptional creations for its 20th anniversary
description: The institution has chosen to display Sarah Bernhardt's gown from 'Ruy Blas,' along with creations by Christian Lacroix and the dazzling red sequined and feathered dress worn by Line Renaud in her Paris and Las Vegas shows.

headline: 'Tourism tends to subject entire regions to its imperatives'
description: According to geographer Rémy Knafou in an interview with Le Monde, online platforms have driven exponential growth in tourism worldwide. This growth serves as a source of wealth creation but also leads to significant dysfunction due to inadequate regulation.

headline: How Le Monde's London correspondent almost became a Reform UK candidate in Britain's local elections
description: After being approached multiple times, Le Monde's London correspondent recounts the questionable recruitment tactics of the far-right party. Her misadventure reflects Reform UK's efforts to try to take London from Labour on May 7.

Global backslide in women's rights did not begin with Trump
description: On March 9, the US refused to sign off on the conclusions of the UN Commission on the Status of Women, the main global women's rights forum. This was the first time since the forum's creation in 1946, and it reflects the growing threat that conservative ideologies pose to feminist achievements.

headline: Oil traders' windfall profits highlight paradoxes of Swiss model
description: Geneva is full of rumors about the record profits made by oil market players. The legal framework in which they operate has drawn criticism and sparked calls for tighter regulation.

headline: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
description: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?
